Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Normative Process Description The process always begins with Receive order After receiving the order next step is Check inventory before any decisions about payment authorization or fulfillment are made If inventory sufficient then process continues as follows after check inventory do Authorize Payment then Package Items then Ship Order then Close Order The sequence becomes Receive Check Authorize Payment Package Ship Close If inventory is not enough after Check Inventory go directly to Cancel order Then Close order That gives a short sequence Receive Check Cancel Close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Additionally normative constraints Authorize Payment should only occur after Check Inventory and before Packaging Package Items should only occur after Authorization and before Shipping Ship Order should occur after Packaging and never after cancellation Close Order is final activity

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order No violations Sequence matches expected process for sufficient inventory So case 1 is correct

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2 Receive order Check inventory Package items Authorize payment Ship order Close order violation Package items performed before authorization The normative description says that Authorize payment should only occur after check inventory and before packaging

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 3 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Cancel order Ship order Close order the process branch is inconsistent Payment was authorized before the cancellation decision According to normative description if inventory check indicates insufficient stock then the process should go directly to cancel order without payment authorization

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 3 another violation Ship order occurs after cancellation which violates Ship order should only occur after packaging and never after a cancellation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 4 Receive order Check inventory Cancel order Close order Sequence for insufficient stock branch should be Receive Check Cancel Close and indeed that is what we see here So Case 4 seems to conform perfectly

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 5 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Ship order Close order violation packaging step is missing According to the normative description after Authorize Payment should come Package items then Ship Order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus answer Case 1 compliant Case 2 violates due to out of order execution of payment authorization and package items Case 3 violates because it includes payment authorization before cancellation in a cancellation scenario and also ships after cancellation Case 4 compliant Case 5 violates because package items is missing before shipping
